verses.1: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
verses.3: to receive instruction in wise dealing,
verses.4: to give prudence to the simple,
verses.5: that the wise man may hear, and increase in learning;
verses.6: to understand a proverb and parables,
verses.7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge,
verses.8: My son, listen to your father’s instruction,
verses.9: for they will be a garland to grace your head,
verses.10: My son, if sinners entice you,
verses.12: Let’s swallow them up alive like Sheol,
verses.14: You shall cast your lot among us.
verses.15: my son, don’t walk on the path with them.
verses.16: for their feet run to evil.
verses.17: For the net is spread in vain in the sight of any bird;
verses.18: but these lay in wait for their own blood.
verses.19: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain.
verses.20: Wisdom calls aloud in the street.
verses.21: She calls at the head of noisy places.
verses.22: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity?
verses.24: Because I have called, and you have refused;
verses.25: but you have ignored all my counsel,
verses.26: I also will laugh at your disaster.
verses.27: when calamity overtakes you like a storm,
verses.28: Then they will call on me, but I will not answer.
verses.30: They wanted none of my counsel.
verses.31: Therefore they will eat of the fruit of their own way,
verses.32: For the backsliding of the simple will kill them.
verses.33: But whoever listens to me will dwell securely,
